instructions.purpose: Expose validated commercial next actions to the conversational model without scripting the dialogue.

actions.objective: Ask for exactly one unresolved fact only when its answer materially changes the recommendation, architecture, scope, estimate, delivery risk, or next executable action. Supply a concise semantic rationale from the conversation, durable state, or authoritative knowledge; a literal source quotation is not required.
When an exact total is grounded and the customer is ready, let the customer choose among the enabled payment options.
When an exact total and payment option are grounded, give the customer the validated checkout action and obtain a payment commitment.

rules: Treat a fact as a material blocker only when a concise semantic rationale explains why its answer materially changes the recommendation, architecture, scope, estimate, delivery risk, or next executable action.
Do not convert already answered, generic, or merely useful discovery details into mandatory blockers.
Do not repeat a qualifier already requested unless the customer asks for clarification.

When scope is sufficiently understood and no grounded total exists, provide a grounded solution hypothesis and seek one decision that materially determines scope, delivery, or the next executable action. Do not create a pseudo-action for future preparation.
Request one concrete customer commitment that advances the transaction.
